author: @philclancaster
text:
Some thoughts on chlorophyll.  I once had a go at making a Cyanotype photo using chlorophyll instead of potassium ferrocyanide.  It worked reasonably well with a 3 hr exposure in bright sunlight. the unexposed areas stayed deep green and the exposed went a light green-brown.
I used spinach which i understand has a high concentration of the best type of chlorophyll and finely chopped it. I then put this into acetone for 48 hrs in the dark. The deep green extract was then separated from the leaves and used to soak paper.  Once dried in the dark I made a photo by placing objects on the paper and leaving in direct bright sun light. 
I found that if i added a little copper acatate the chlorophyll it went from deep green to a bright green and no longer faded in the sun (only tested for 3 hrs). My idea was to fix the photo by washing it in copper acetate.  
As life is busy i did get time to repeat the experiment and fix the final photo. 

The reason to share this is:
 1. To let you know acetone is an effective way to extract chlorophyll
2. Adding copper acatate stops fading in sunlight or at least slows it.  This may improve the life of the solar cell. 

I have read the colour change in the chlorophyll is due to an ion exchange between the magnesium in the chlorophyll and the copper in the salt but i'm not a chemist so not sure.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Phil Lancaster that is very very useful mate - thanks for that - i will use that on the very next set of experiments - thanks very much

author: @DiyEcoProjects
text:
Hi Robert, ... just got back from a festival at samhain honouring the ancestors. I can see youve been busy with another project. Well done mate, looks realy interesting. I can see why youve been up so late. "Grow your own solar panel" will be the cool title lol.

hmm... so youve sent me on a google journey to read up on some things and found this. Thought this might be useful. -  "Chlorella - a single cell-celled algae has the highest chlorophyll content of any plant" which is a health food suppliment.

... also your "Paper Thin Aluminium Air Battery Made With A Deep Eutectic Solve"... just thinking maybe your conductive ink being black placed on the back could help the "leaf solar cell" absorb more sunlight somehow

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: DiyEcoProjects could be - nice thoughts mate
